bugs.title: Token expiry too short for agentic tests
bugs.description: The JWT token expired mid-test (within ~5 minutes of issuance based on timing), requiring creation of a new user to continue. The token's exp claim showed only ~4 hours validity but expired prematurely, possibly due to clock skew or iat being set in the past.
bugs.expected: Token should remain valid for the full 1-hour access token lifetime
bugs.actual: Token expired after approximately 3-4 minutes of testing

ux_issues.description: The cross-cutting religious etiquette comparison response was truncated by MAX_TOKENS before covering Buddhist temple etiquette in Myanmar. The AI tried to produce a comprehensive comparative table but exceeded the response length limit, resulting in the response ending with whitespace padding and an error message.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider increasing MAX_TOKENS for planning mode responses, or instruct the AI to be more concise with comparative answers to avoid truncation.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: No itinerary items created across 5 planning messages
ai_behavior_issues.context: Despite extensive discussion about Jerusalem sites, Saudi Arabia logistics, Myanmar considerations, and cross-cutting etiquette, the AI never called create_itinerary_items to add structured items to the itinerary. The user explicitly asked about day-by-day plans but the AI only provided text-based advice.

Across 5 planning messages covering Israel (history/religion), Saudi Arabia (culture), and Myanmar (history/culture), the AI never called suggest_expert to hand off to a location or theme specialist. Topics like Jerusalem religious sites and Saudi cultural etiquette are ideal candidates for expert handoff (e.g., Israel+history, Saudi Arabia+food for dinner recommendations).

ai_behavior_issues.issue: MAX_TOKENS truncation on comparative etiquette response
ai_behavior_issues.context: When asked for a comparative overview of dress codes across all three countries, the AI produced an extremely verbose response that hit the token limit. The response ended with hundreds of whitespace characters and a truncation error, never reaching the Buddhist temple etiquette section for Myanmar.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI demonstrated excellent cultural sensitivity and factual accuracy across all three complex destinations. It correctly handled Temple Mount/Haram al-Sharif dual naming, accurately stated that abayas are no longer legally required for foreign women in Saudi Arabia, provided a balanced and factual assessment of Myanmar's post-coup situation without editorializing, and confirmed Bagan temple climbing restrictions. Israel safety was discussed frankly without fear-mongering. However, the AI failed to create any itinerary items or trigger expert handoffs, and one response was truncated by MAX_TOKENS. The cultural depth and factual accuracy were impressive, but the lack of structured planning output was a significant gap.
